description: Maps federal agencies to their procurement portals and spending patterns beyond SAM.gov. v2 enumerates 13 public DOE national-lab bid boards + 4 SAM-routed labs, NASA/JPL, agency vendor portals, and prime-contractor supplier portals; new secondarySources carry platform/listsOpps/access. Companion strategy doc: docs/strategy/OPPORTUNITY-DATA-LANDSCAPE.md

secondarySources.name: Defense Logistics Agency Internet Bid Board System (DIBBS)
secondarySources.notes: Product-specific, commodities and supplies

DEPARTMENT OF DEFENSE.tips: Most DoD IT and professional services go through GSA Schedule or SeaPort. Get on a vehicle first.

secondarySources.name: Army Contracting Command Portal
secondarySources.notes: Information only, not a bidding portal

DEPARTMENT OF THE ARMY.tips: USACE (Corps of Engineers) uses MATOC and regional SATOC vehicles extensively.

secondarySources.name: Navy Electronic Commerce Online (NECO)
secondarySources.notes: Requires CAGE code registration

DEPARTMENT OF THE NAVY.tips: SeaPort-NxG is essential for Navy professional services. 45% of Navy spending goes through this vehicle.

DEPARTMENT OF THE AIR FORCE.tips: Air Force has more open competition than other services. Watch SAM.gov closely.

secondarySources.name: GSA Advantage
secondarySources.notes: Online shopping for GSA Schedule holders

secondarySources.name: GSA eBuy
secondarySources.notes: RFQ portal for Schedule holders

GENERAL SERVICES ADMINISTRATION.tips: GSA Schedule is the gateway. 60% of GSA's own spending and billions in assisted acquisitions go through Schedule.

secondarySources.name: VA eCMS
secondarySources.notes: VA-specific procurement portal

DEPARTMENT OF VETERANS AFFAIRS.tips: VA strongly prefers SDVOSB set-asides. Get CVE verified for massive advantage.

secondarySources.name: HHS Grants.gov
secondarySources.notes: Primary portal for HHS grants

DEPARTMENT OF HEALTH AND HUMAN SERVICES.tips: NIH CIO-SP vehicles dominate HHS IT spending. Also watch NIH SBIR/STTR for R&D.

secondarySources.name: CBP Business Connection
secondarySources.notes: CBP vendor management portal — registration/market-research surface

DEPARTMENT OF HOMELAND SECURITY.tips: EAGLE II is the primary IT vehicle. Getting on is competitive but worthwhile.

DEPARTMENT OF JUSTICE.tips: DOJ uses GSA Schedule heavily. More open competition than defense agencies.

DEPARTMENT OF STATE.tips: State Department often requires security clearances. International work opportunities.

secondarySources.name: Treasury SBECS
secondarySources.notes: Small Business Enterprise Contacts System — vendor registry

DEPARTMENT OF TREASURY.tips: IRS is the biggest buyer. Financial systems expertise valued.

secondarySources.name: USDA Grants Portal
secondarySources.notes: Agricultural research grants

DEPARTMENT OF AGRICULTURE.tips: USDA Forest Service is a major buyer. Rural development focus.

DEPARTMENT OF COMMERCE.tips: NOAA and Census Bureau are key buyers. Weather and data analytics focus.

DEPARTMENT OF LABOR.tips: Workforce development and training opportunities. Good small business set-asides.

DEPARTMENT OF TRANSPORTATION.tips: FAA and FHWA are major buyers. Infrastructure focus.

secondarySources.name: DOE National Labs (index)
secondarySources.notes: Labs have independent procurement — enumerated below

secondarySources.name: Sandia (SNL) — Business Opportunities Website
secondarySources.notes: BOW public to view; iSupplier registration to respond

secondarySources.name: Los Alamos (LANL) — Procurement Opportunities
secondarySources.notes: Competitive opps >$250K on public Ariba Discovery profile

secondarySources.name: Lawrence Livermore (LLNL) — Opportunities
secondarySources.notes: Construction opps table public; larger buys on SAM.gov

secondarySources.name: Oak Ridge (ORNL) — Business Opportunities
secondarySources.notes: Public to view + subscribe; Ariba/SAM registration to bid

secondarySources.name: Pacific Northwest (PNNL) — Solicitations
secondarySources.notes: Solicitations >$250K posted externally; ASP registration to bid

secondarySources.name: Lawrence Berkeley (LBNL) — Project Opportunities
secondarySources.notes: Public project-opportunity listing

secondarySources.name: Brookhaven (BNL) — Bid Specs
secondarySources.notes: Bid Specs page public; major buys on SAM.gov; vendor registry to bid

secondarySources.name: NREL — Purchasing & Subcontracting Opportunities
secondarySources.notes: Public RFP/RFQ/SSN/RFI/NOI table

secondarySources.name: Ames Laboratory — Procurement
secondarySources.notes: Public current-solicitation list

secondarySources.name: Princeton Plasma (PPPL) — Opportunities
secondarySources.notes: RFPs listed publicly; Princeton supplier portal registration to bid

secondarySources.name: SLAC — Upcoming Contract Opportunities
secondarySources.notes: Upcoming opps public; SAM + PeopleSoft registration to bid

secondarySources.name: Jefferson Lab (JLab) — Solicitation Bulletin Board
secondarySources.notes: Solicitation Bulletin Board public; bidder portal registration to bid

secondarySources.name: Savannah River Nat'l Lab (SRNL) — Active Procurement Opportunities
secondarySources.notes: Active opps public; Oracle Fusion account to transact

secondarySources.name: Argonne (ANL) — Procurement & Contracts
secondarySources.notes: Own site routes suppliers to SAM.gov for opps

secondarySources.name: Fermilab (FNAL) — Supplier Portal
secondarySources.notes: Opportunities via SAM.gov; portal opps invite-only

secondarySources.name: Idaho (INL) — Vendor Portal
secondarySources.notes: Login-gated; opps via SAM.gov; SAM registration prerequisite

secondarySources.name: NETL — Solicitations
secondarySources.notes: GOGO lab — no private portal; routes to SAM.gov/FedConnect/eBuy/SEWP

DEPARTMENT OF ENERGY.tips: GOCO/FFRDC labs post SUBCONTRACTS on their own portals (not SAM.gov) — 13 of 17 have a public bid board. Argonne/Fermilab/INL/NETL route to SAM.gov. Identifiable platforms to normalize: SAP Ariba (LANL, ORNL), Oracle iSupplier (Sandia), Oracle Fusion (SRNL), PeopleSoft (SLAC, LBNL, PNNL).

secondarySources.name: Dept. of Education — MySBCX
secondarySources.notes: Small business contact/market-research portal

DEPARTMENT OF EDUCATION.tips: Heavy grants focus. Educational technology and assessment services.

ENVIRONMENTAL PROTECTION AGENCY.tips: Environmental consulting and remediation. Good small business opportunities.

secondarySources.name: NSPIRES
secondarySources.notes: NASA research announcements and proposals

secondarySources.name: NASA SEWP
secondarySources.notes: IT hardware and solutions

secondarySources.name: NASA JPL — Acquisition Opportunities
secondarySources.notes: Caltech-operated FFRDC — subcontracts NOT on SAM.gov by default. Current RFI/RFP table + acquisition forecast

secondarySources.name: NASA JPL — Small Business Opportunities
secondarySources.notes: RFI/RFP list + forecast for small business

NATIONAL AERONAUTICS AND SPACE ADMINISTR.tips: SEWP V is huge for IT. SBIR/STTR program is excellent for R&D companies.

SMALL BUSINESS ADMINISTRATION.tips: Small agency but sets small business policy. 8(a) and HUBZone programs.

SOCIAL SECURITY ADMINISTRATION.tips: Large IT infrastructure needs. Disability determination services.

primeSupplierPortals.description: Prime-contractor supplier/subcontract portals — where teaming & subcontract opportunities on large primes live (outside SAM.gov). Registration-gated. Source: GovCon Chamber Federal Directory (~80 primes enumerated); representative set below.

gsa_schedule.name: GSA Multiple Award Schedule (MAS)
gsa_schedule.description: Base requirement for most federal contracting. Gateway to government sales.
gsa_schedule.howToGet: Apply at GSA eOffer. 6-12 month process. Need 2 years commercial sales history.

idiq_vehicles.name: Indefinite Delivery/Indefinite Quantity (IDIQ)
idiq_vehicles.description: Pre-competed contract vehicles for specific services/products.
idiq_vehicles.howToGet: Watch SAM.gov for solicitations. Highly competitive, requires past performance.

bpa.name: Blanket Purchase Agreement (BPA)
bpa.howToGet: Usually awarded from GSA Schedule or open competition.

newContractors: Get GSA Schedule first - it's the foundation
Start with small agencies that have more open competition
Build past performance before pursuing IDIQ vehicles
Consider subcontracting to gain prime experience
establishedContractors: Pursue IDIQ vehicles in your NAICS codes
smallBusiness: Get 8(a), WOSB, SDVOSB, or HUBZone certification if eligible
Target agencies with strong small business programs (VA, SBA)
Joint ventures can access larger opportunities
